summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the 'conjugate method' for evaluating limits?

Rationalizing by multiplying by the conjugate (e.g., вИЪa + вИЪb) eliminates radicals and resolves 0/0 forms involving square roots.

What is 'algebraic simplification' in the context of limits?

Algebraic simplification in limits refers to techniques like factoring, expanding, or rationalizing to rewrite 0/0 forms into expressions that can be evaluated.

What is a 'common factor' in the context of limit evaluation?

The common factor (e.g., x вИТ a) in both numerator and denominator causes both to be zero at x = a, creating the 0/0 form.

What is the 'special trigonometric limit' lim(xвЖТ0) sin x / x?

lim(xвЖТ0) sin x / x = 1 is a fundamental trigonometric limit, proven using the Squeeze Theorem.

An engineer computes lim(tвЖТ3) (t¬≤ вИТ 6t + 9)/(t¬≤ вИТ 9) for a stress analysis. What is the result?

(tвИТ3)¬≤/[(tвИТ3)(t+3)] = (tвИТ3)/(t+3). At t = 3: 0/6 = 0.

A financial analyst evaluates lim(nвЖТвИЮ) (1 + 1/n)вБњ to determine continuously compounded growth. What is this famous limit?

lim(nвЖТвИЮ)(1 + 1/n)вБњ = e вЙИ 2.71828..., the base of natural logarithms and foundation of continuous compounding.

A biologist models enzyme reaction rate as R(x) = (5x вИТ 5вИЪx)/(x вИТ 1) near x = 1. What is lim(xвЖТ1) R(x)?

Factor: 5(x вИТ вИЪx)/(x вИТ 1) = 5вИЪx(вИЪx вИТ 1)/[(вИЪxвИТ1)(вИЪx+1)] = 5вИЪx/(вИЪx+1). At x=1: 5(1)/(1+1) = 5/2.

A student encounters lim(xвЖТ0) (1 вИТ cos x)/x¬≤ on a practice exam. What is the answer?

Multiply by (1+cos x)/(1+cos x): sin¬≤x/[x¬≤(1+cos x)]. As xвЖТ0: [sin x/x]¬≤ ¬Ј 1/(1+cos x) = 1 ¬Ј 1/2 = 1/2.

A civil engineer needs lim(hвЖТ0) [вИЪ(16 + h) вИТ 4]/h to find the rate of change of a bridge cable's length. What is this limit?

Rationalize: h/[h(вИЪ(16+h)+4)] = 1/(вИЪ(16+h)+4). At h=0: 1/(4+4) = 1/8.

A data scientist evaluates lim(xвЖТвИЮ) (5x¬≥ вИТ 2x)/(x¬≥ + 100) to determine the asymptotic behavior of an algorithm. What is the limit?

Divide by x¬≥: (5 вИТ 2/x¬≤)/(1 + 100/x¬≥) вЖТ 5/1 = 5 as x вЖТ вИЮ.

An AP student encounters lim(xвЖТ0) tan(2x)/x. What is the value?

tan(2x)/x = [sin(2x)/cos(2x)]/x = [sin(2x)/x] ¬Ј [1/cos(2x)] = 2 ¬Ј 1 = 2.

A chemistry student needs lim(xвЖТ1) (x¬євБ∞ вИТ 1)/(x вИТ 1) to model a reaction rate. What is the answer?

(xвБњ вИТ 1)/(x вИТ 1) = xвБњвБї¬є + xвБњвБї¬≤ + ... + x + 1. At x = 1 with n = 10: sum of 10 ones = 10.

A pharmaceutical researcher evaluates lim(xвЖТ0) [e^x вИТ 1]/x to study drug absorption kinetics. What is this fundamental limit?

lim(xвЖТ0)(eЋ£ вИТ 1)/x = 1. This is the definition of the derivative of eЋ£ at x = 0, since d/dx(eЋ£)|вВА = eвБ∞ = 1.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A student encounters lim(xвЖТ0) (1 вИТ cos x)/x¬≤ on a practice exam. What is the answer?

Multiply by (1+cos x)/(1+cos x): sin¬≤x/[x¬≤(1+cos x)]. As xвЖТ0: [sin x/x]¬≤ ¬Ј 1/(1+cos x) = 1 ¬Ј 1/2 = 1/2.
